items.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn’t
items.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

items.title: Telegram ban in India sparks a rush to VPNs, rival apps
items.summary: Telegram argues India should block specific content, not an entire platform used by millions.

items.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
items.summary: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

items.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
items.summary: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.

items.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
items.summary: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

items.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
items.summary: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

items.title: The 11 standout startups from YC’s Demo Day, according to VCs
items.summary: TechCrunch spoke to investors to find the hottest startups in the Spring 2026 YC batch. Some of them commanded valuations of over $175 million, VCs said.

items.title: Rivian owners file lawsuit alleging false promises on self-driving features
items.summary: Plaintiffs in the class -action complaint allege Rivian falsely promised for years it would bring hands-free driving to its first-generation R1 vehicles.

items.title: Almost half of US singles feel negatively about AI in dating, Match says
items.summary: About 47% of singles look negatively at the use of AI in dating -- but many dating app users are open to AI helping with profile punch-ups and conversation starters.

items.title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
items.summary: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

items.title: Mivo’s new app takes a mindful approach to managing screen time
items.summary: Notably, unlike other apps that might just try to pull you away from your phone, Mivo lets the user decide if they want to continue, encouraging users to become more aware of how and why they’re using it instead.

items.title: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid
items.summary: FERC told grid operators to give data centers a fast lane for interconnections, but it failed to address electricity supply shortages.

items.title: The smartphone era created an attention crisis — slow tech is fixing it
items.summary: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

items.title: Texas government data breach allowed hackers to steal 3 million driver’s licenses and passports
items.summary: A data breach involving government-issued ID documents affects over 3 million people in Texas.

items.title: ‘Queer Eye’ life coach Karamo Brown launches Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone
items.summary: After spending a year and a half focusing on his own journey — from fitness and nutrition to meditation, sobriety, relationships, and personal growth — Brown wants to help others do the same.

items.title: Apple opens up App Store to new competition in Brazil
items.summary: Apple’s grip on iPhone app distribution is loosening in another major market: Brazil.

items.title: MapTap, a daily geography game, is my new Wordle
items.summary: MapTap is a phone game that will make you feel smarter after you play it.

items.title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
items.summary: The startup trains embodied AI and world models using Medal’s dataset of 2 billion videos per year from 10 million monthly active users.

items.title: A tech worker-backed PAC is bringing a $5M knife to Big Tech’s $100M gunfight
items.summary: Guardrails positions itself as a populist political movement that runs on small donations from people in the trenches of the AI boom.

items.title: Spotify’s reserved ticket sales to music superfans are now going live
items.summary: Spotify is launching "Reserved," a new system that will hold two concert tickets for an artist's superfans before they're on sale to the public.
